name: Camp McCain POW Branch Camp
description: The Camp McCain POW Branch Camp was established in 1943 to hold captured Afrika Korps soldiers, operating under Geneva Convention protocols for prisoner-of-war treatment. Prisoners were typically assigned to agricultural or labor details under armed guard, helping address the acute wartime manpower shortage in rural America. The compound consisted of wood-frame barracks surrounded by double apron barbed-wire fencing with guard towers at the corners; water and sanitation facilities were constructed to minimum Army standards. Post-war, the structures were demolished and Camp McCain is now Grenada County Reservoir public land. Metal detectorists have recovered German belt buckle 'Gott Mit Uns', aluminum mess kit fragments, and 1943 wheat pennies from the old compound footprint.
source: NARA Record Group 389 (POW Operations) / Army Provost Marshal General Records
